reviewer: retrospective critique backfill (code-grounded + existing conformance suite), revision 1

rubric.evidence: worker.js loads a REAL transformers.js summarization pipeline (pipeline('summarization', 'Xenova/long-t5-tglobal-base', {device:'wasm', dtype:'q8'})) and calls pipe(text, gen) on-device with real max_new_tokens/min_new_tokens/num_beams/no_repeat_ngram_size/length_penalty; streaming via TextStreamer gives the model's own decode cadence. Conformance calls-real-inference (source) + loads-clean (console-clean) tested pass; io-shape-semantic manual-evidenced. No measured latency fabricated.
rubric.notes: As with distilbart, per-token timing is only meaningful for greedy decoding (beams===1); beam search reorders tokens so the streamer is skipped and only aggregate timing is reported — disclosed in the code.

rubric.evidence: Expected output = summary_text. worker.js returns {summary, inTokens, outTokens, inChars, outChars, intervals, beams, ms} from the real run; the page streams the partial text and draws the per-token decode timeline. io-shape-semantic conformance assertion covers a real summary from the input.
rubric.notes: HONEST MODEL NOTE (from the worker's own header): this is the BASE LongT5 checkpoint, NOT a summarization fine-tune. It was chosen because Pegasus (the requested XSum-family model) has no transformers.js ONNX build, and LongT5 is the closest DISTINCT, browser-runnable relative — pretrained with Pegasus's gap-sentence objective and transient-global attention for long inputs. Because it is not summarization-fine-tuned, its gists lean EXTRACTIVE (it lifts the most salient sentence(s)) rather than fluently abstractive. This is a genuine quality limitation, disclosed in the code and worth surfacing to users.

rubric.evidence: At-a-glance declares Xenova/long-t5-tglobal-base (linked, 5 occurrences), the summarization task, q8 dtype, WASM backend, ~335 MB and Apache-2.0 license — matching models.json. declares-model-id/task/backend/quantisation conformance assertions tested pass.
rubric.notes: LongT5-TGlobal base: transient-global attention lets it read far longer inputs than DistilBART's ~1024-token BART window — the reason this fills a distinct architecture family. Inference off the main thread.

rubric.evidence: The page uses the shared createModelLoader auto-init (modelId Xenova/long-t5-tglobal-base, q8, ~335 MB, requiresWebGPU:false); a valid current cached copy auto-initialises with no Load click. shared-auto-init-loader conformance assertion tested pass.
rubric.notes: ~335 MB — the never-silently-re-download guarantee and Clear-cache control matter; surfaced in at-a-glance.

rubric.evidence: runStatus role=status aria-live; streamed partial summary in an aria-live region; recap posts step/done progress; worker errors surface on the page; shared loader adds checking→initialising→ready + Retry. live-status-region + errors-on-the-page tested pass; honest-progress-retry-offline / runs-without-webgpu-fallback manual-evidenced.

Real, labelled controls: text <textarea>, three range sliders (Max length, Min length, Beams) with live value readouts, a Summarize button and a recursive recap. All keyboard-operable. controls-operable conformance assertion is manual-evidenced.
rubric.notes: Same proven control pattern as the distilbart-summarization demo.

rubric.evidence: No canned output: streamed summary, token counts, per-token intervals and recap chain are all from the real pipeline. no-fake-output conformance assertion is manual-evidenced.

Buttons named (buttons-have-names tested pass); status role=status aria-live; summary region aria-live; sliders labelled with live values; page lang=en; WCAG-AA light+dark. images-have-alt tested pass.
rubric.notes: The per-token timeline is a <canvas>; consulted expose-canvas-content-to-browser-features. The token counts / intervals are also shown as text; an explicit canvas aria-label is a recorded open item.

rubric.evidence: responsive-desktop/responsive-mobile (no-horizontal-overflow) conformance assertions present; design-system panels + width-constrained canvas. models.json support is needs-review on both classes (automated pre-scan flagged sub-24px tap targets), pending an agent matrix pass to confirm slider/button tap sizes and flip to ok.

Generation runs in the Web Worker; tokens stream to the main thread which appends text and draws a bounded canvas timeline. inference-off-main-thread conformance assertion tested pass; inp-stays-low manual-evidenced.
rubric.notes: The larger model + longer inputs make a run slower than distilbart, but the work is off-thread so it does not block INP.

guidanceConsulted.query: keep INP low move heavy work off main thread web worker
guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Run heavy generation off the main thread and stream progress; keep INP low.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — summarization + recap run in the worker; TextStreamer streams tokens so the UI stays responsive during a long decode.
guidanceConsulted.evidence: worker.js off-thread streaming; inference-off-main-thread conformance assertion tested pass

guidanceConsulted.query: expose canvas content to assistive technology accessible name
guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Give canvas content a meaningful accessible name / text equivalent.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: partially applied — the timeline canvas is mirrored by a text readout of token counts and per-token intervals; an explicit canvas aria-label is a recorded follow-up.
guidanceConsulted.evidence: text readout duplicates the canvas; canvas aria-label open item

guidanceConsulted.query: accessible form labels text input textarea range slider
guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Label every control; echo slider values in text; keyboard-operable.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — Max/Min length and Beams sliders labelled with live value readouts; source textarea labelled.

guidanceConsulted.query: responsive control panel without horizontal overflow flex grid
guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Fluid layout with wrapping; keep the canvas within its container.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — design-system panels; the canvas is width-constrained so it never forces horizontal overflow.
guidanceConsulted.evidence: responsive assertions present; support needs-review pending agent matrix pass

openQuestions: Should the page state MORE prominently that this is the base (non-fine-tuned) LongT5, so users understand why summaries read extractively rather than abstractively?
Would a summarization fine-tune of a long-context model (if a browser ONNX ever ships) be worth a NEW sibling demo to contrast base-vs-fine-tuned behaviour?

followUpGoals.goal: Surface the base-checkpoint / extractive-gist caveat in the page UI (not only the code comment) so users calibrate expectations, and add an aria-label to the timeline canvas.
Run the mobile+desktop responsive matrix, confirm/raise tap targets, and flip models.json support to ok.

summary: Retrospective, code-grounded revision-1 critique for longt5-summarization (Xenova/long-t5-tglobal-base, summarization, q8, ~335 MB, Apache-2.0). Real summarization: worker.js runs the transformers.js pipeline off the main thread with genuine length/beam controls, streams tokens via TextStreamer, caps output to ~90% of input, and offers recursive recap. Chosen as the closest DISTINCT browser-runnable relative to the requested-but-unbuildable Pegasus (shares Pegasus's gap-sentence objective + transient-global long-context attention). Auto-init via createModelLoader; role=status aria-live states; canvas decode timeline mirrored by text. Verified against the immutable conformance suite (calls-real-inference, loads-clean, declares-*, inference-off-main-thread, shared-auto-init-loader, live-status-region, buttons-have-names tested pass; io-shape/parity/inp manual-evidenced). No fabricated numbers. Honest weaknesses flagged: this is the BASE checkpoint (not a summarization fine-tune) so gists lean extractive — a genuine quality limitation disclosed in code and worth surfacing in the UI; timeline canvas lacks an aria-label (mitigated by text); ~335 MB heavy on mobile; models.json support needs-review pending a tap-target matrix pass.
